entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud: Professional Services Customer Proj...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there is no delivered standard functionality to split “List Price” and “Discount” into separate accounting lines at invoice level. By default, condition types like PSP0/PCP0 influence planned revenue only, not the billed revenue. To achieve the business requirement, you need to use extensibility either create custom condition types mapped to revenue and discount accounts, or leverage the Cloud Application Studio / Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for accounting determination.

If accounting-level separation is mandatory, implement custom condition types with separate GL accounts. If reporting-only is acceptable, use Group Reporting derivation rules. Meanwhile, monitor SAP release notes for native support of discount postings.

entry.title: Sharing SAP S/4HANA Data with Databricks Using BDC Connect and Delta Sharing
entry.content:
In our scenario, data originates from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, is replicated into SAP Datasphere, and then securely shared to Databricks using SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect and Delta Sharing. This approach enables zero‑copy data access in Databricks while keeping data governed and managed within SAP.

This blog focuses on the end‑to‑end data replication and sharing flow, along with key prerequisites, monitoring steps, and performance considerations.

High Level Flow:

S/4HANA - >  Replication Flow (CDS-based) -> Datasphere Object Store (Parquet files) -> Managed Data Product -> BDC Connect -> Delta Sharing -> Databricks
 
At a high level:
  • SAP S/4HANA replicates data using released CDS views
  • Data is stored in Datasphere Object Store as Parquet files
  • A managed data product is created on top of this data
  • The data product is shared externally using BDC Connect
  • Databricks consumes the shared data via Delta Sharing

Data Replication from S/4HANA to SAP Business Data Cloud:

The ABAP Data Integration framework—specifically the ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS)—is used to replicate data from S/4HANA into SAP Business Data Cloud.

Key points:

  • Only released CDS views can be replicated
  • The available CDS views depend on:
    • Your S/4HANA release
    • The data products shipped with that release
  • Replication supports:
    • Initial load
    • Change Data Capture (CDC) for deltas

Pre-Requisites:

Platform & Connectivity

The following setup steps are mandatory but not covered in detail in this blog:

  • Enable BDC Cockpit
  • Establish BDC Connect between SAP BDC and Databricks
  • Create a Formation in SAP for Me Marketplace
  • Ensure Datasphere Object Store is available and configured (Delta Sharing does not work with HANA tables)
SAP S/4HANA Prerequisites:

Before starting replication, review the SAP prerequisite note for your S/4HANA version:

  • SAP Note: 3500131 – ABAP Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud

ABAP Integration is available starting from the following releases (subject to change, always verify the note):

  • SAP S/4HANA 2021
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 07
  • SAP S/4HANA 2022
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 05
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 03
  • SAP S/4HANA 2025
    • Initial release
  • The replication framework must be active and CDC jobs must be healthy and running.  Important SAP Note 3561739 - "ABAP Data Integration - Technical Jobs" and set jobs in scope. 

Required Jobs in SJOBREPO:

After configuration, verify that the following jobs are in scope and active (green status) in transaction SJOBREPO. Activation may take some time.

Technical Job Definition Description
/LTB/JOB_DISPATCHERCentral cross‑application job scheduler
SAP_DHAFR_WRITER_START_DAEMONMonitors delta replication queue and triggers delta replication
SAP_DHBAS_RETENTION_JOBExecutes data retention actions
SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMONTriggers initial load and calculation jobs
SAP_DHCDC_DISPATCHERCentral dispatcher for CDC jobs
SAP_DHCDC_HOUSEKEEPERNightly housekeeping job

Note:
SAP_DHCDC_HOUSEKEEPER is automatically added when replication starts and removed when replication is not used.

Activating Data Packages in BDC Cockpit

  1. Log in to BDC Cockpit
  2. Navigate to Intelligent Applications and Data Packages
  3. Verify that the required applications are part of your formation
  4. Select the relevant data packages and click Activate

Activation duration depends on:

  • Data volume
  • Number of CDS views
  • Available parallel jobs

BDC Cockpit Screenshot:

You should see the relevant intelligent apps and data packages for the applications included in your formation. Based on your requirement and your S4HANA version, you can activate the relevant data packages.
You should be able to see the Activate button on the top right corner

The activation takes time depending on the amount of data to be transferred, some performance techniques are listed at the bottom of this blog.

Monitoring and Troubleshooting:

You can monitor the for any error logs using DHCDCMON tcode > application logs, there are chances that you might run into errors as mentioned in this note 0003610376    Cannot start ACP daemon (client XXX) - ABAP CDC Engine.

Ensure that the R_JR_BTCJOBS_GENERATOR job has been running recently for the client (check for this job in active/finished status). This job should run every hour for each active client.

Check whether the SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMON job is stuck in 'released/scheduled' status. If so, and the R_JR_BTCJOBS_GENERATOR job has been running, delete this instance of the SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMON job before waiting up to an hour for it to be run (job may need to be deactivated and reactivated in SJOBREPO after deleting its stuck instance in SM37).

Data Package Activation Status

After activation starts or completes:

  • Status is visible in BDC Cockpit
  • Data products that require a higher minimum S/4HANA version will fail activation and are clearly marked
 

 

Performance Considerations:

Initial Load Parallelization

  • Initial load of CDS views is parallelized by default
  • Parallelism depends on job configuration in transaction DHADM

Relevant SAP Note:

  • 3223735 – Control of parallelism
    • Parameters: TRANSFER_MIN_JOBS / TRANSFER_MAX_JOBS
    • Job: /1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA

Delta Loads

  • Delta (CDC) loads are not parallelized
  • They run continuously based on data changes

Final Step:

To delta share the data products to databricks, navigate to Catalog and Marketplace in your BDC cockpit and you should be able to see the active data products, select the data product.

Click Share icon:

Add Target and choose the BDC connect details to Databricks and share

Databricks Consumption:

Once shared:

  • Databricks users can see the data product in their catalog
  • They can mount or query the shared dataset using Delta Sharing
  • No physical data copy is required
  • Data scientists can immediately perform analytics and ML workloads

Conclusion

By combining SAP S/4HANA replication, SAP Business Data Cloud managed data products, and BDC Connect with Delta Sharing, organizations can enable secure, governed, zero‑copy analytics in Databricks. This architecture minimizes data duplication, reduces operational cost, and ensures SAP remains the system of governance while enabling advanced analytics and machine learning.

entry.title: Re: Business Area (GSBER) not updated after Asset change...
entry.content: Yes, this is standard SAP behavior. Once the Business Area (GSBER) is derived during PR creation, it is not automatically re-derived if the Asset (ANLN1) is changed later. There is no delivered configuration to force re-derivation. SAP recommends using a BAdI such as ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (which you also indicated) if you need dynamic updates.

entry.title: Re: Event-Based Revenue Recognition - How to consider tax...
entry.content:
SAP deliberately excludes tax postings from EBRR accruals to remain compliant with legal requirements. Taxes are only posted at the time of billing. There is no standard setting to force VAT postings during revenue recognition accruals. Even if you try to extend account determination, the system will not generate tax lines because the tax procedure is tied to billing documents, not accrual postings.

My advice is to treat this as a compliance safeguard rather than a missing configuration. If your business insists on seeing tax impact earlier, the safest path is to build a reporting simulation rather than altering FI postings.

entry.title: Re: SAP FI Asset accounting treatment with CO WBS AUC
entry.content:
Your two proposed options are both valid within SAP’s standard framework, and the way you’ve structured them is consistent with how SAP expects WBS-driven investment flows to work.

  • Option 1 (Standard PS Process): Best for global consistency, audit traceability, and detailed project reporting.
  • Option 2 (Lightweight): Best for operational efficiency in entities that don’t need deep project-level analysis, but carries risk of reduced transparency.

So the recommended approach can be

If global harmonization and audit traceability are top priorities → go with Option 1.

If operational simplicity for U.S. entity is critical and project-level analysis is less important → Option 2 can work, but you must document the rationale and ensure compliance with accounting standards.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC 6.0 – Fiscal Year Variant Change ( 5-4-4 → Ca...
entry.content:
Modifying an existing year‑dependent Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) in ECC with historical postings is considered high‑risk and generally not recommended. The safer practice is to create a new FYV aligned to calendar months and migrate balances during the S/4HANA conversion. SAP does not provide a supported way to “re‑open” past periods (like 27–30 June) without redefining the FYV, and audit/governance teams usually prefer a clean cutover rather than retroactive changes.

Though I am well aware of your comments "We are looking for real‑world ECC experience, not just OB29 theory.", below are the key risks of modifying existing FYV

1) Historical Postings:
Changing OB29 for a year‑dependent FYV affects all prior years. Reports, reconciliations, and asset histories may misalign.

2) Integration Impact:
FI, CO, MM, SD, RE‑FX, Asset Accounting, BW, and Payroll postings all reference fiscal periods. A change can break reconciliation across modules.

3) Audit Concerns:
Retroactive changes to fiscal calendars are difficult to defend. Auditors typically expect continuity of past reporting.

4) Technical Limitations:
SAP does not support “in‑place” modification of FYV with history; OB29 changes are meant for new fiscal years only.

Check the below comparison of options

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors: Adding Custom Roles in Performance Route Map.
entry.content:
This is not really about adding Custom Roles. Second Manager is a standard role used in Compensation Planning processes.

Of course, if you do not have the Compensation module, you can customize this role as you wish. However, if you do have it, it does not make sense to refer to it as a Custom Role.

SAP should allow Employee Central customers to add Job Relationship roles so they can be selected in the Route Map. If you agree, please vote for the following Customer Influence idea:

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

entry.title: Copy control for item category HUPB is missing in LF > IV - what to do with open deliveries?
entry.content:
Hi all

In SAP public cloud the copy control for HUPB is missing for IV billing documents originating from a LF delivery document. 

After raising a ticket with SAP for this, they advised me to create a Feature request to have this Copy control installed. But at the same time to also raise a question here on how to deal with the open deliveries. 

Through the app 'Create billing documents' the users create invoices from deliveries. But in the intercompany flow these deliveries remain open as HUPB items cannot be invoiced. And as long as the copy control is not there the list will keep increasing. 

Is there a known workaround we could do to have these deliveries closed, so they do not remain open? Or any other solution to this problem?

Kr

entry.title: Support Needed: OAuth2 SAML Bearer Destination connection to Successfactor Issue in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Dear Connections,

I’m reaching out regarding an issue we are currently facing with the OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion destination setup in SAP BTP for establishing a connection with SuccessFactors.

Background

We are working on integrating our CAP-based application deployed on SAP BTP with SuccessFactors using a destination configured with OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion authentication.

Current Status

  • The destination works successfully with Basic Authentication
  • However, when switching to OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion, the connection fails

Error Details

  • We are encountering the error:
    “No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'.","stacktrace":["Error: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'."”

What We Have Verified

  • Destination is correctly configured in BTP
  • OAuth client is created in SuccessFactors
  • JWT token is available and being passed from CAP (via request headers)
  • User attributes (logonName) are available in the request context
  • CAP application is correctly bound to XSUAA and Destination services

Suspected Areas

  • OAuth configuration mismatch 
  • Certificate/trust configuration between BTP and SuccessFactors
  • SAML assertion generation issues via XSUAA

Request

Since this setup involves multiple components (CAP, XSUAA, Destination service, and SuccessFactors), I wanted to check if anyone have experience with similar OAuth2 SAML Bearer configurations in BTP.

It would be really helpful if you could:

  • Suggest any checks or troubleshooting steps we might be missing

CAPM Code:

const { executeHttpRequest } = require("@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client");
const { getDestination } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity');
module.exports = async function (request) {
         let payload = request.data;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Payload: " + JSON.stringify(payload));
        const workflowPath = `odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true`;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Path: " + workflowPath);
        payload["__metadata"] = { "uri": "Position" };
const destination = await getDestination({
        destinationName: "sfAdminDestination",
        userJwt
        });
const workflowRes = await executeHttpRequest(
            destination,
            {
                method: "POST",
                url: "odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true",
                data: payload,
                headers: {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
                    "Accept": "application/json"
                }
            }
        );

}
Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story: How to send notification alert via email w...
entry.content:
@S0019834417 

You could explore usage of ValueChangeOverThreshold api and isSendEmail API of class NotificationOptions to achieve this.

https://help.sap.com/doc/1639cb9ccaa54b2592224df577abe822/2026.2/en-US/index.html#DataChangeInsightType_P_static_ValueChangeOverThreshold

https://help.sap.com/doc/1639cb9ccaa54b2592224df577abe822/2026.2/en-US/index.html#NotificationOptions_PisSendEmail

Alternatively you could put the logic of your red threshold explicitly in the script. Something like below.

// 1. Identify recipients (SAC User IDs)
var receivers = ["USER_ID_1", "USER_ID_2"];
var resultSet = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet();
var thresholdBreached = false;

for (var i = 0; i < resultSet.length; i++) {
    var val = parseFloat(resultSet[i]["@MeasureName"].formattedValue);
    if (val < 70000) { // Your red threshold condition explicitly defined
        thresholdBreached = true;
        break;
    }
}

if (thresholdBreached) {
    Application.sendNotification({
        "title": "Threshold Breach Detected",
        "content": "A value has entered the RED zone. Please check the dashboard.",
        "receivers": receivers,,
        "isSendEmail": true
    });
}

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: PRODUCTION ORDERS MUST BE SETTLED AGAIN TWO MONTHS AF...
entry.content:
Here is the breakdown which would clear your doubts

Moving average price (V):
Every goods movement (GR, GI, settlement) can potentially change the material’s valuation. If settlement is missed and consumption happens, the system posts variances directly to P&L instead of adjusting the material value. That’s why you saw the costs bypass inventory and hit expense accounts.

Late settlement issue:
When users forget to settle, the production order variances are not capitalized into the material. Once consumption has already occurred, the system cannot retroactively adjust the moving average price for those past postings. It only posts the variance to P&L.

MR21 price change:
MR21 directly changes the material price in accounting. When the user changed the price on 31/03/2026, SAP reopened the possibility for settlement because the valuation basis had changed. The system then allowed the production order to be settled again, but instead of adjusting inventory (since consumption had already happened), it posted the difference to P&L. That’s why you saw the accounting document generated at that later date.

Why this happens:
With moving average price, SAP expects settlement to happen promptly after technical completion. If settlement is delayed and inventory has already been consumed, the link between production variances and inventory valuation is broken. MR21 essentially reset the valuation, which made the system reprocess the settlement, but by then the only option was to push the difference into profit and loss.

Having said that, the unusual behavior is not a bug but a consequence of using price control V with delayed settlements. MR21 changes the valuation layer, which re-enables settlement, but since the manufactured stock was already consumed, SAP cannot adjust inventory retrospectively and posts to P&L instead.

If your client wants to avoid this, the recommended practice is to use standard price (S) for manufactured items. That way, variances are always posted to price difference accounts, and you don’t run into these timing issues with moving average price.

entry.title: Re: change in Input variables which i am taking from CDS...
entry.content:
@kandagatla-nagaraju 

You could use a Date input control of type range with system Date calendar.

  1. Create Input Control: In the Builder panel of a chart or table, add a filter on your Date dimension.
  2. Set Range: Select Filter by Range.
  3. Define Current Date: In the "Current Date" dropdown, select Create Current Date Input Control.
  4. Configure:
    • Name: Give it a title (e.g., "Select Current Date").
    • Default: Keep it set to System Date / Period to ensure it always defaults to today's actual date when the story is opened.
    • Granularity: Choose "Day" to enable a daily calendar picker

It should create something like below.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Financial Closing cockpit (no?) value added for u...
entry.content:
Why This Happens
  • Design Philosophy: FCLOCO was built to enforce governance and auditability, not flexibility. It prioritizes compliance and control over user experience.
  • Templates: Task list templates are structured but not intuitive. Customization requires technical effort and often feels restrictive.
  • Tracking: Even if tasks are executed in SAP, FCLOCO doesn’t automatically update completion status unless integrated properly. This leads to manual updates.
  • Reporting: Reporting is limited. There is no global dashboard view unless you build custom CDS views or embed analytics.

 

Given below are Good Practices which you can consider
 
Automate Task Completion:
Link FCLOCO tasks directly to SAP transactions/programs so completion updates automatically.
Example: Assign the program for foreign currency valuation or depreciation run to the task—once executed, FCLOCO marks it complete.
 
Use Hierarchical Task Lists:
Organize tasks by country, company code, or process area. This makes navigation easier and reduces clutter.
 
Enhance Reporting:
Build custom SAP Fiori apps or CDS views to visualize closing status globally.
Few clients integrate FCLOCO with SAP Analytics Cloud for dashboards showing completion by country, overdue tasks, and bottlenecks.
 
Limit Administration:
Avoid overloading FCLOCO with micro-tasks. Group small steps into larger milestones to reduce administrative burden.
 
Change Management:
Train users on why FCLOCO matters (audit trail, compliance, global visibility). Without this, they see it as “extra work.”
 
Position FCLOCO as a control tool, not a replacement for Excel. Many companies still use Excel for local tracking but rely on FCLOCO for audit and global oversight.
 
So the bottom line message is you are not wrongly using FCLOCO; your experience is widely shared. The key is to automate task completion, reduce template complexity, and build reporting layers on top. FCLOCO is best treated as a compliance cockpit, while Excel or BI tools remain the “friendly” operational trackers.

entry.title: Set Field Mandatory for Profitability Segment in Post General Journal Entries
entry.content:
Hi SAP community,

My business needs to set up mandatory for 2 fields Product and Ship-to Party in Profitability Segment of line item for specified G/L accounts in Post General Journal Entries.

According SAP team's advice, I tried to create rule in Manage Substitution and Validation Rules - Journal Entries application.

 

However, there are some issues and and also this rule doesn't meet requirement:

  • This rule can not block totally posting in Post General Journal Entries application: after entering data of header and line items - without entering data of Assign Profitability Segment, I clicked Post, a pop up announces like the screenshot below. And Post button is disabled.

But when I retried to post by click any field and Enter again, Post button is active and I'm able to post normally.

  • The activation of this rule does not achieve the intended requirement of blocking journal entry postings in the Post General Journal Entries app. Moreover, it unexpectedly blocks other business processes, such as create invoice, create sales order.
 
  • Finally, the data range defined for creating the rule does not seem to be correct. For example, for the Ship-to Party, the range is defined as numeric only; however, in practice, all business partners start with an alphabetical character followed by numbers.
 
I'd appreciate it if the experts could review this case and advise whether there is a suitable way to create a rule that fulfills my requirements, or if there is an alternative approach to enforce these two fields as mandatory.
 
Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: I need to extract the AIM Configuration Filter Parame...
entry.content:
Hello Shruthi,

In case of Technical systems, the monitoring filter parameters are saved in the table /IMA/COLLCONFIG.
You can specify the category with 'CATEGORY_ID', e.g. IDOC, WS, PIAB_MBMSG, etc.
You can check the LMDBID of Technical systems referring to the KBA 3647048.

In case of Cloud systems, the monitoring filter parameters are saved in the table /IMA/CLD_FILTERS.
You can specify the monitoring use case with 'CATEGORY_KEY', e.g. SAP_ARIBA_CIG_MESSAGE, HCI, etc.
LMDBID of Cloud systems can be checked from the table /IMA/CLD_CONFIGS or /IMA/CLDPUSH_CFG(push based services).

Hope above helps you.
Best regards,
Claudia

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud and Azure integration for SSL (Let...
entry.content:
Hi Viktor,

thanks for your reply, this is very helpful.

Just to clarify one point: based on your explanation and the provided APIs, it seems that the certificate rotation process is expected to be handled by an external system that invokes these APIs.

In a scenario where only SAP Commerce Cloud is available (no additional services or middleware), is it correct to assume that there is no built-in way to schedule or execute this process internally (e.g. via a job within Commerce Cloud itself)?

entry.title: Re: Table names and string translation
entry.content:
There are two options here.

First option: You create your own variable where you check the document language and the parameter ObjectId@ and select the document name accordingly.

Second option: you use the "Default elements ​​for Crystal reports" table, place all texts with translations there, and retrieve these values ​​using the stored procedure "TmSp_GetDefaultElementsforCR".

entry.title: Re: AI-Assisted Ratings for Inferred Skills
entry.content:
Hi @RobertaP 

The "Assisted Ratings for Inferred Skills" feature enables AI-assisted ratings specifically for inferred skills. This feature infers skill ratings based on the titles and descriptions of learning items and programs, matching them to the appropriate proficiency levels in the Attributes Library. The AI-generated skill ratings are displayed on the Assisted Skills Inference page, where you can review the inferred skills and their ratings and then associate them with relevant items and programs. Once linked, these skills and ratings appear in the Attributes section of the Items and Programs pages. Basically, this enhancement improves skill inference by automatically identifying both skills and their ratings, eliminating the need for manual entry.

entry.title: Re: handle dynamic field control
entry.content:
Hi 

I don't know a good method, so this is one of those no-answer answers. My suggest is to maybe make multiple dynpros instead of one dynpro handling multiple scenarios with  dynamic field control.

I have some programs where it has become very complex with a lot of rules for single fields and also for fields inside table controls.
The reason I ended in this complex situation is classic. The business requested a program to handle something for production of -  lets say bikes. "We must be able register how much plastic is used in the handlebars, saddle and 2 wheels".

They are happy and use the program for 2 years. Then they come back with an idea "we also want to use this program for kick scooters without a saddle, so to prevent errors you must hide this field when it is kick scooters".

Next year they want to use the program for go-karts, so now we need four fields for registering wheels data, b only when it is go-karts.
Next they want to register both plastic and steel, but only for bikes and for green kick scooters.
..and so on.

From the beginning and with the first new requirements to the program it seemed to be an easy fix to use dynamic field control, but in the end it turned out to be very complex to maintain. 
If I from the beginning had know the full requirements, I would have made one dynpro for bikes, one for saddles and one for kick scooters. The rest of the program with pai/pbo moduls, sub routions, class methods etc. I would have structured with reusable objects, so I would for example only have one plastic registration mechanism reused in all 3 dynpros.

So, I'm sorry this answer is not good ideas how to make dynamic field control simple. It is an advice to only use dynamic field control in simple scenarios.

I am excited to follow this thread to see if others have truly good advice about how to handle complex dynamic field control.

Best regards 
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire about the account determination logic for subcontracting goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Background

We are currently using subcontracting processes for both:

  • Finished Goods (FERT)
  • Semi-Finished Goods (HALB)

The business requirement is to separate G/L accounts for:

  • Goods receipt (inventory offset account)
  • Component consumption

We have maintained Automatic Account Determination (GBB) with different:

  • General Modifications (ZOB, VBO)
  • Valuation Classes (e.g., 7900 for HALB, 7920 for FERT)

2. Observations

Based on our testing:

  1. For normal goods receipt (e.g., movement type 501):
    → GBB (ZOB) is applied and G/L accounts can be controlled
  2. For component consumption:
    → GBB (VBO) is applied and G/L accounts can be changed successfully
  3. For subcontracting goods receipt:
    → The inventory offset account is always posted to G/L 50300000
    → Changes in GBB settings (ZOB / VBO) are not reflected
    → Only the consumption part follows VBO settings

3. Questions

We would like to clarify the following:

  1. What is the standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt?
  2. Is the G/L account (e.g., 50300000) determined by:
    • OBYC / GBB configuration, or
    • Other internal logic (e.g., subcontracting or valuation-related logic)?
  3. Is there any configuration (e.g., SSCUI) to control or override this account?
  4. Is this behavior standard in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

4. Expectation

We would like to confirm whether it is possible to:

  • Assign different G/L accounts for subcontracting goods receipt
    (e.g., separate accounts for FERT and HALB)

If not, we would appreciate clarification on:

  • Standard design limitations
  • Recommended alternatives

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Michi

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud: Project Manager Report
entry.content:
This issue has been resolved in the meantime. With Release 2502 SAP provided the Business Catalog Enterprise Projects - Project Management (SAP_PPM_BC_PROJ_MGMT_PC).

 
Highlights of this Business Catalog:
  • Access to projects is restricted based on the assignment of users to individual projects in the application.
  • With this catalog, users can only access a project if they are assigned to it.
  • They are authorized to use the functionality described above if they are assigned to the project as project manager.

entry.title: Minimum charges per waybill in FO
entry.content:
Hello,

We have way bill number per ship to party and many ship to party material can go in one truck.

The minimum weight per waybill should be 20KG. Every ship to has a different way bill.

The way bill is stored in delivery. The way bill number and gross weight will be read from delivery document (or we need to create custom field for way bill and its corresponding weight in FO.

basic freight charges are Rs 5 per kg.

so if for one way bill A has 10 kg and another way bill is 5 kg. Then system should charge minimum 20kg per way bill.

so in this case Basic charge for Way bill A will be 20 * 5=100

basic charge for way bill B is 20 * 5= 100

total charges 200 Rs (100 for A and 100 for B)

However if Way bill A is 30 kg and way bill B is 40 kg.

then total charges for way bill A will be 30 X 5= 150
total charges for way bill B will be 40 x 5 =200

total charges =150+200 = 350 in FO.

how can we meet the requirement for charging min 20 kg / waybill in above case.

entry.title: Re: Standard logic used to determine the G/L account for...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt is determined by standard valuation logic rather than the GBB settings you maintain. The system posts the inventory offset for subcontracting GR to a fixed account (e.g., 50300000) based on valuation area and account determination rules, while the component consumption follows GBB (VBO). This behavior is standard and cannot be overridden via SSCUI for subcontracting GR

The limitation in Public Cloud is SSCUI allows you to configure GBB for consumption but does not allow separate BSX accounts for subcontracting GR by material type (HALB vs FERT). The system design enforces a single inventory offset account for subcontracting GR.

Standard logic for Subcontracting GR

  • Normal GR (e.g., 501): Uses GBB with modification (ZOB) and valuation class to determine the offset account.
  • Component Consumption: Uses GBB (VBO) and valuation class, allowing differentiation between HALB and FERT.
  • Subcontracting GR: The inventory offset account is determined by valuation grouping code and transaction key BSX (stock account), not GBB. This is why you always see postings to 50300000 regardless of valuation class.

What you can do is

Separate Consumption Accounts:
Continue using GBB (VBO) with valuation classes to differentiate HALB vs FERT component consumption.

Inventory Offset Accounts:
Cannot be split by material type for subcontracting GR in Public Cloud.

Alternatives:

  • Use different valuation areas if business requires separate inventory accounts.
  • Build reporting logic (e.g., CDS views) to track subcontracting GR by material type, even if the posting account is the same.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud and Azure integration for SSL (Let...
entry.content:
Hi @stefaniaZ ,

Additional information of blog related to Automate TLS certificate renewal - an introduction to SAP Commerce Cloud Portal API
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/automate-tls-certificate-renewal-an-introduction-to-sap-commerce-cloud/ba-p/14278025 

In this blog article we have seen how to use the Commerce Cloud Portal public API to retrieve information about certificates, check for expiring certificates, renew them using an external tool, upload the renewed certificates and exchange them in all assigned endpoints.
All steps were done using standard command line tools and bash scripting, making it easy to integrate into existing automation pipelines.Of course, the actual certificate renewal step will depend on your setup and might be quite different from the example shown here.

SSL Certificate Management in SAP Commerce Cloud:
In a scenario where only SAP Commerce Cloud is available (without additional services or middleware), there is no built-in way to schedule or execute SSL certificate renewal processes internally within Commerce Cloud itself.

Key Points:
1. No Native Auto-Renewal for Custom Certificates

SAP Commerce Cloud does not support automatic renewal for custom SSL certificates.
Only the out-of-the-box (OOTB) certificates provided by SAP Commerce Cloud are automatically renewed
Custom certificates require manual renewal and upload through the Cloud Portal.

2. OOTB Certificate Auto-Renewal Process

An internal job runs which will check OOTB certificates assigned to endpoints


3. Manual Process Required for Custom Certificates
  Custom certificates must be renewed manually through the SAP Commerce Cloud Portal.

 The process involves:
Obtaining new certificate files from your Certificate Authority (CA) provider
Creating/updating the SSL certificate in the Cloud Portal.
Attaching the certificate to the appropriate endpoints

4. No Internal CronJob Solution:

While SAP Commerce Cloud has robust CronJob capabilities for various business logic tasks, there is no built-in CronJob or internal mechanism specifically designed for SSL certificate renewal.
CronJobs in Commerce Cloud are designed for business operations like catalog synchronization, data imports/exports, and scheduled tasks, not for infrastructure-level certificate management.

Recommendation:
To automate certificate renewal, you would need to use external solutions such as:

The SAP Commerce Cloud Portal API for programmatic certificate management.
External middleware or automation scripts that interact with the Cloud Portal API.
Integration with certificate management services outside of Commerce Cloud.

If you require auto-renewal functionality for custom certificates, SAP recommends submitting an improvement request through the SAP Customer Influence portal.
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003262364 : How to Submit an Improvement Request 

I hope this additional info would be helpfull.

Regards,
Prasanth
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Using an Unbundled Apache Tomcat - What is Your confi...
entry.content:
Hi Michael,

The Tomcat documentation suggests that the default behavior of Tomcat is to collapse the slashes to a single slash:
https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/context.html#Naming

This is the parameter:
allowMultipleLeadingForwardSlashInPath

This attribute has a default value of false which enables the collapsing of multiple / characters. To disable this behaviour, set this attribute to true.

Is it possible somehow in your standalone tomcat that this has been set to true?

I checked our context.xml (both the Tomcat one and the BOE application one) and I don't find this parameter specified, which means we'd use the default behavior of collapsing the // to /

entry.title: Re: S4H00 Certification
entry.content:
HI @CRazouk 
Since S4H00 (SAP S/4HANA Overview) is a foundational learning course, it’s best used as a starting point to decide which role‑based SAP certification fits your goals. 

Below please find some recommendation to help you choose find the right certification after completing S4H00.

Recommended Certifications Based on Your Role or Interest

1. Functional Consultant (Business Processes)
If you’re interested in specific business functions, choose a module‑based certification:

  • Finance

    • SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting
    • Exam codes typically start with C_TS4FI_*

  • Controlling

    • SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Controlling
    • Exam codes typically C_TS4CO_*

  • Sales

    • SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Sales
    • Exam codes typically C_TS4SD_*

  • Sourcing & Procurement

    • SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement
    • Exam codes typically C_TS4MM_*

2. Cloud‑Focused or Line‑of‑Business Roles

If your interest is in SAP S/4HANA Cloud rather than on‑premise:

  • SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
  • Exam codes often start with C_S4C_*

3. Technical or System Roles
If S4H00 sparked your interest in system architecture or operations:

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP S/4HANA System Administration
  • SAP Certified Development Associate – ABAP with SAP S/4HANA

4. General or Entry‑Level Path (Still Exploring)
If you’re new to SAP and not ready to specialize yet:

  • Continue with role‑based learning journeys in SAP Learning Hub
  • Explore additional overview or beginner courses before committing to an exam

How to Proceed

  1. Decide which role aligns best with your career goals
  2. Visit the SAP Certifications catalog 
  3. Filter by:
    • Solution: SAP S/4HANA
    • Level: Application Associate
  4. Prepare with recommended learning journeys
Visit our SAP Certification practical exams Step-by-step guide for more details.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAC Email notifications
entry.content:
We’re looking for a solution that can send an email notification to users when a KPI value drops by a specified amount.

We’ve already tried using the Application.sendNotification() API in SAP Analytics Cloud, but it only generates in-app SAC notifications and does not send emails.

We need a way to ensure that users receive these notifications via email, specifically on a designated day of each month.

entry.title: Did not receive badge for 'ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial'
entry.content:
Hi,

I noticed that other participants who completed the tasks have already received the challenge completion badge. I completed all four tasks, as well as the optional one, by the end of March 2026. After finishing each weekly task, I also posted the required screenshots as proof of completion.

I’m trying to understand why the badge hasn’t been awarded to my profile. If this was an oversight, I kindly request that the badge be granted. If I made any mistakes in the process, I would appreciate guidance so I can correct them and learn for future challenges.

regards.

entry.title: Is the Rehire Coordinator Group Required in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding for Customers Without EC
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are an SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding customer without Employee Central (EC). Our rehire identification and processing are done manually in SAP HCM once a recruiter identifies the candidate as a rehire.

Recently, we experienced a case where a candidate triggered Onboarding but no BPE (Business Process Engine) rehire confirmation error was generated. In another case, the BPE error did occur. To resolve the inconsistency, SAP Support advised us to create the following dynamic group:

SAP_ONB2_RehireCoordinator
(Manage Onboarding Dynamic Groups)

However, based on SAP documentation and previous guidance, my understanding is that:

  • ONB2 rehire functionality relies heavily on EC data
    (personId, employmentInfo, termination status, etc.)
  • Without EC, ONB has no native way to detect rehires, so the Rehire Coordinator Group might not have functional value.

This raises several questions:

❓My Questions to the Community

  1. Is the Rehire Coordinator Group actually required for customers who do not use Employee Central?
  2. What is the SAP standard (OOB) behavior for rehire handling in ONB when EC is not part of the landscape?
  3. Is the group creation merely a workaround to eliminate BPE rehire‑check errors, or does it serve a functional purpose?
  4. How should non‑EC customers properly manage rehires to avoid BPE errors or unexpected ONB triggers?
  5. Are there official SAP guidelines or best practices for Rehire Management specifically for non‑EC implementations?

Any insights, experiences, or references from SAP experts or other customers would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAC Email notifications
entry.content:
@Himanshu_Sharma_26 

Are you setting the flag for issendemail to true? You can refer answer to a similar topic below.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-story-how-to-send-notification-alert-via-email-when-measure-in/qaq-p/14370423

// 1. Identify recipients (SAC User IDs)
var receivers = ["USER_ID_1", "USER_ID_2"];
Application.sendNotification({
        "title": "Threshold Breach Detected",
        "content": "A value has entered the RED zone. Please check the dashboard.",
        "receivers": receivers,,
        "isSendEmail": true
    });

 Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Is there any limit for creation of no. of company cod...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no strict system limit on how many company codes you can create. However, creating 1000+ company codes is not a practical or recommended approach.

In real scenarios, this will lead to:

  • Performance and usability issues in Fiori apps
  • Very complex reporting and intercompany processes
  • Higher implementation and maintenance effort (time and cost)

There is also a relevant SAP KBA 3427985, which highlights limitations when handling large numbers of company codes in applications.

Further See Here

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/mulitple-country-version-and-multiple-company-codef/qaq-p/12305089 

Regards

Hi,

Click on more link relevant apps will be display you can add further apps.

 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Is the Rehire Coordinator Group Required in SAP Succe...
entry.content:
@el_aagnote 

  1. Is the Rehire Coordinator Group actually required for customers who do not use Employee Central?Gopi :SAP_ONB2_RehireCoordinator is OOTB group will create once you enabled ONB2.0 switch in provisioning.
  2. What is the SAP standard (OOB) behavior for rehire handling in ONB when EC is not part of the landscape?Gopi :It is OOTB group required for external users to be created properly in system .for your case i would recommend keep this group but do not add any users in it . utilize rehire rule scenario to assign to respective team members from different groups(rule Scenario :Assign Responsible Group for Onboarding Rehire Check)
  3. Is the group creation merely a workaround to eliminate BPE rehire‑check errors, or does it serve a functional purpose?Gopi : OOTB group and it should be present in system.
  4. How should non‑EC customers properly manage rehires to avoid BPE errors or unexpected ONB triggers?Gopi: refer my response in pt2.
  5. Are there official SAP guidelines or best practices for Rehire Management specifically for non‑EC implementations?Gopi : Refer blog by @Angela_Romano on best practives:https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/best-practices-for-onboarding-and-miniec-with-a-focus-on-offboarding-and/ba-p/14019854

Hope above answer your queries

Thanks,

Gopi

entry.title: Re: Print Handling Unit Label During Creation of Inbound...
entry.content:
Dear @giorgio_mazzeo7,

Good to hear, it works. The reference to Process in the 2508 documentation should be understood as referring to the process-specific enablement/configuration for automatic HU label printing in inbound automatic packing, rather than to a universally visible and mandatory OPD parameter in the basic Handling Unit determination tables

Best Regards,

Dawid

Specifically, I have the following questions:

  1. Sourcing Integration: How does the Procurement Expansion Pack integrate with SAP Ariba Sourcing to initiate RFI and RFP processes from purchase requests created in SAP Ariba Buying?
